2603.17948.title: VideoAtlas: Navigating Long-Form Video in Logarithmic Compute
2603.17948.paper_summary: Extending language models to video introduces two challenges: representation, where existing methods rely on lossy approximations, and long-context, where caption- or agent-based pipelines collapse video into text and lose visual fidelity. To overcome this, we introduce \textbf{VideoAtlas}, a task-agnostic environment to represent video as a hierarchical grid that is simultaneously lossless, navigable, scalable, caption- and preprocessing-free. An overview of the video is available at a glance, and any region can be recursively zoomed into, with the same visual representation used uniformly for the video, intermediate investigations, and the agent's memory, eliminating lossy text conversion end-to-end. This hierarchical structure ensures access depth grows only logarithmically with video length. For long-context, Recursive Language Models (RLMs) recently offered a powerful solution for long text, but extending them to visual domain requires a structured environment to recurse into, which \textbf{VideoAtlas} provides. \textbf{VideoAtlas} as a Markov Decision Process unlocks Video-RLM: a parallel Master-Worker architecture where a Master coordinates global exploration while Workers concurrently drill into assigned regions to accumulate lossless visual evidence. We demonstrate three key findings: (1)~logarithmic compute growth with video duration, further amplified by a 30-60\% multimodal cache hit rate arising from the grid's structural reuse. (2)~environment budgeting, where bounding the maximum exploration depth provides a principled compute-accuracy hyperparameter. (3)~emergent adaptive compute allocation that scales with question granularity. When scaling from 1-hour to 10-hour benchmarks, Video-RLM remains the most duration-robust method with minimal accuracy degradation, demonstrating that structured environment navigation is a viable and scalable paradigm for video understanding.
2603.17948.paper_summary_zh: 擴展語言模型到視頻引入了兩個挑戰：表示，其中現有方法依賴於有損近似；以及長上下文，其中基於字幕或代理的管道將視頻壓縮為文本並失去視覺真實性。為了克服這些挑戰，我們介紹了 \textbf{VideoAtlas}，這是一個任務無關的環境，用於將視頻表示為一個層次網格，該網格同時是無損的、可導航的、可擴展的，且不需要字幕和預處理。視頻的概覽可以一目了然，任何區域都可以遞歸放大，並且對視頻、中間調查和代理的記憶使用相同的視覺表示，從而消除了端到端的有損文本轉換。這種層次結構確保了訪問深度隨著視頻長度的增長僅以對數方式增長。對於長上下文，遞歸語言模型（RLMs）最近為長文本提供了一個強大的解決方案，但將其擴展到視覺領域需要一個結構化的環境來進行遞歸，這正是 \textbf{VideoAtlas} 所提供的。作為馬爾可夫決策過程的 \textbf{VideoAtlas} 解鎖了 Video-RLM：一種平行的主-工作者架構，其中主控協調全局探索，而工作者則同時深入指定區域以積累無損的視覺證據。我們展示了三個關鍵發現：（1）隨著視頻持續時間的增加，計算增長呈對數增長，這進一步受到來自網格結構重用的 30-60\% 多模態緩存命中率的放大。（2）環境預算，其中限制最大探索深度提供了一個原則性的計算-準確性超參數。（3）隨著問題粒度的變化而出現的自適應計算分配。當從 1 小時擴展到 10 小時的基準時，Video-RLM 仍然是最持久的持續時間穩健方法，準確性降級最小，這表明結構化環境導航是一種可行且可擴展的視頻理解範式。
2603.17948.author: Mohamed Eltahir et.al.
2603.17948.authors: Mohamed Eltahir, Ali Habibullah, Yazan Alshoibi, Lama Ayash, Tanveer Hussain, Naeemullah Khan
